# UC-10.18.5 · Broadcom Symantec SEP Network Threat Protection IPS Event Correlation

## Description

SEP’s network layer can block exploits and reconnaissance before payloads touch disk. Correlating repeated IPS-style events by internal host and external peer distinguishes broad scanning from targeted lateral movement precursors.

## Value

Improves network-aware detection at the endpoint tier and prioritizes hosts seeing sustained attack traffic for firewall or EDR follow-up.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm Network Threat Protection events reach Splunk with attacker and victim IP fields. (2) Enrich `remote` with threat intel ASN and reputation. (3) Threshold for burst detection (for example five or more events per pair per day). (4) Join to perimeter firewall logs for corroboration.

## SPL

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype="symantec:ep:security:file" earliest=-24h
| eval desc=coalesce(Event_Description, event_description, _raw)
| where match(lower(desc), "(?i)intrusion|ips|network threat|port scan|denial of service|exploit|network attack")
| eval host=coalesce(Computer_Name, computer_name, host)
| eval remote=coalesce(Remote_IP, remote_ip, Attacker_IP, attacker_ip, src_ip, "")
| stats count values(desc) as event_samples by host remote
| where count>=5
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Table (host, remote IP, count), link analysis diagram, choropleth or geomap if geoip available.

## References

- [Splunk Add-on for Symantec Endpoint Protection](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2772)
- [MITRE ATT&CK — Network Service Scanning](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1046/)
